_comment: Reference template only — not rendered as-is. Copy blocks into a fresh <work_dir>/onepager-spec.json, fill every [bracket], then render with scripts/render_dossier_pydocx.py exactly like the standard dossier-spec.json. See body_template.md 'Exec one-pager variant' for when to use this vs. the standard ≤4-page dossier. Origin: an onepager-spec.json under content/<active>/accounts/<account-slug>/.
closingLine: Internal — [company] leadership only. Not for distribution.

blocks.subline: For [principal name, e.g. the account's CEO] · meeting with [buyer name], [buyer title]

lines: [One-sentence framing: why this meeting is worth the principal's time — the real strategic angle, not a generic intro. State plainly if the target is pre-everything / early-stage / unverified — don't bury that.]

rows: [Their own stated value proposition, quoted verbatim from their site/materials where possible — not your paraphrase.]
[The problem they say they solve, quoted or tightly summarized — include their own stat/proof point if they cite one.]
[The concrete benefit/differentiator they claim, e.g. what makes their mechanism different from the obvious alternative.]
[Plain-English mechanism summary. Flag explicitly what is unverified/unconfirmed publicly — that becomes the open question for the call, not a settled fact.]

blocks.text: [Blunt, evidence-based read on the founder/company track record — funding, prior ventures, traction, verified vs. self-described credentials. Correct any misattribution found during research (e.g. data-aggregator errors) matter-of-factly, without narrating your own research process ('corrected from first pass' etc. — the principal never saw the first pass). Name the genuine positive if there is one, not just the gaps. Keep to 3-5 sentences — this is the whole calibration, not a dossier-length backstory.

lines: [If the principal has ALREADY agreed to take the meeting, do not restate a generic 'recommendation' to take the call — instead give 'What to get from the call': the 2-3 concrete unknowns that determine the outcome, framed as a decision tree ('if X, then Y; if not, there's nothing to pursue'). Only include a take/pass recommendation block if the meeting itself is still undecided.]
